text.title: EVM Metrics and Formulas
text.summary: Core EVM metric definitions, formulas, and interpretation thresholds for objective project performance measurement.

list.item: PV (Planned Value) = BAC x % planned — how much work should be done by now
EV (Earned Value) = BAC x % complete — how much work is actually done (objective measure only)
AC (Actual Cost) — money or effort actually spent to date
BAC (Budget at Completion) — total approved project budget

rule.statement: Use objective % complete only: 0/50/100 rule, milestone-based, or units-completed — never subjective opinion.
rule.rationale: Subjective % complete (team says "80% done") corrupts EV and cascades into false SPI/CPI/EAC values. One inflated EV reading misleads stakeholders for the rest of the project.

list.item: SV (Schedule Variance) = EV - PV; positive = ahead, negative = behind schedule
CV (Cost Variance) = EV - AC; positive = under budget, negative = over budget
SPI (Schedule Performance Index) = EV / PV; >1 ahead, <1 behind
CPI (Cost Performance Index) = EV / AC; >1 under budget, <1 over budget

rule.statement: Color-code: SPI/CPI in [0.95, 1.05] = green; [0.85, 0.95) = yellow; below 0.85 = red.
rule.rationale: Consistent thresholds across all reporting periods allow stakeholders to compare status at a glance without re-interpreting scales each time.

section.title: Forecasting Metrics

list.item: EAC (Estimate at Completion) = BAC / CPI — predicted final cost assuming current efficiency continues
ETC (Estimate to Complete) = EAC - AC — additional cost needed to finish
VAC (Variance at Completion) = BAC - EAC — expected final over/under run
TCPI (To-Complete Performance Index) = (BAC - EV) / (BAC - AC) — required future efficiency to finish on budget

rule.statement: Flag TCPI > 1.10 as likely unrecoverable; escalate to sponsor for re-baseline or scope reduction decision.
rule.rationale: TCPI > 1.10 means the remaining work must be completed 10% more efficiently than the project has averaged — statistically improbable without a structural intervention.

example.code:
Month 3 of 5, BAC = $100,000: PV = $50,000 (50% of BAC planned) EV = $40,000 (40% complete × BAC) AC = $55,000 (actual spend)
SV = EV - PV = -$10,000 (behind schedule) CV = EV - AC = -$15,000 (over budget) SPI = EV / PV = 0.80 (20% slower than planned) CPI = EV / AC = 0.73 (27% more expensive per $ of work)
EAC = BAC / CPI = $137,000 (predicted final cost) ETC = EAC - AC = $82,000 (still to spend) VAC = BAC - EAC = -$37,000 (over-run) TCPI= (100k-40k)/(100k-55k) = 1.33 → unrecoverable; escalate

section.reference: Pure EVM calculator: WP inputs to full metric set
Three-point EAC Monte Carlo under CPI uncertainty
